codeReview.summary: The feature correctly chose the low-risk documentation option, but the new guide turns the still-ambiguous iOS target-membership story into a claimed CocoaPods/codegen mechanism that the reviewed repo and React Native 0.85 scripts do not substantiate. Because this feature's whole purpose was to make that mechanism trustworthy for future workers, I am failing it on documentation correctness.

issues.description: The new note states that `use_native_modules!` / `use_react_native!` autolink `ios/EnboxMobile/**/*.mm` into `Pods-EnboxMobile` and therefore that `RCTNativeSecureStorage.mm` / `RCTNativeCrypto.mm` are compiled through the Pods target (`.factory/library/ios-target-membership.md:25-32,63-69`). The reviewed evidence does not support that claim: `node_modules/react-native/scripts/codegen/generate-artifacts-executor/generateRCTModuleProviders.js:71-77` only uses `modulesProvider` to emit module-name -> class mappings, `node_modules/react-native/scripts/codegen/templates/ReactCodegen.podspec.template:59-60` and `ReactAppDependencyProvider.podspec.template:25` compile generated code only, and this repo has no podspec or other build-file reference to `RCTNativeSecureStorage.mm` / `RCTNativeCrypto.mm` while `ios/EnboxMobile.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj` still contains no entries for them. As written, the document replaces an acknowledged ambiguity with a likely-wrong implementation story, so it does not yet provide safe guidance for future RN/iOS work.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The new `ios-target-membership.md` guidance is still easy for future workers to miss because the required shared-state doc is `architecture.md`, and that architecture doc does not reference the new file.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/liran/src/enboxorg/mobile/.factory/skills/mobile-feature-worker/SKILL.md:20-22` names `architecture.md`, `environment.md`, and `user-testing.md` as the baseline library docs; `architecture.md` contains no `ios-target-membership` reference, so the new note is only discoverable if a worker manually scans `.factory/library/` or the gotchas doc.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The handoff reports `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`, but the transcript skeleton does not show the required `environment.md` read even though the worker ran install/test-style verification commands.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/liran/src/enboxorg/mobile/.factory/skills/mobile-feature-worker/SKILL.md:28` says to read `environment.md` if the worker will run install/test flows. The transcript skeleton for session `75b86d6f-127b-4bf5-bdd7-6138fb036e4b` includes `bun run verify` and `gh run list --workflow=ci.yml ...` but no `Read` of `/home/liran/src/enboxorg/mobile/.factory/library/environment.md`, while the handoff still sets `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`.

summary: Fail: the feature added the requested shared-state note, but the note asserts an unproven/likely-incorrect CocoaPods target-membership mechanism for `NativeSecureStorage` and `NativeCrypto`. I also recorded two shared-state follow-ups: the new note is not linked from the mandatory architecture doc, and the worker marked the skill procedure as fully followed despite skipping the required `environment.md` read for its verification flow.
